CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Have Execution Time Limits Enforced
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Runtime Security**. Verifies that each AI agent has execution time limits enforced at the process or orchestrator level. Without timeouts, a runaway agent — caused by an infinite reasoning loop, deadlock, or adversarially crafted input — will run until the host is restarted, consuming CPU, memory, and downstream API quotas indefinitely.
Risk: Agents without execution timeouts are vulnerable to resource exhaustion attacks driven by adversarial inputs (e.g., prompt injection that triggers recursive tool calls). In multi-tenant environments, an unbounded agent can affect service availability for other tenants. Timeouts also limit the blast radius of a compromised agent that establishes long-running exfiltration connections.

Code.Other:
1. Set a maximum execution timeout in the agent orchestration framework (e.g., LangChain agent max_iterations + timeout, CrewAI task timeout).
2. Enforce an outer deadline at the platform level: Kubernetes activeDeadlineSeconds for Jobs, AWS Step Functions state machine timeout, Lambda function timeout.
3. Implement watchdog processes that terminate agents exceeding their allocated time budget.
4. Log and alert when a timeout is triggered so recurring violations can be investigated.

Recommendation.Text: Configure execution timeouts both at the agent framework level and at the platform level (e.g., Kubernetes activeDeadlineSeconds). Alert on timeout events to detect adversarial or misconfigured agents.

Notes: ASPM-064 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
